steps.title: Map the two-part structure
steps.content: The passage first discusses two cultural centers operated by tribes: the Riverbend Cultural Center and a Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa center. It then contrasts both with non-Indigenous museums that display Indigenous artifacts differently.

steps.title: Reject structures not present
steps.content: Choice A invents a particular shared set of artifacts. Choice B focuses on audience responses, which are not discussed. Choice D claims an old-versus-new strategy, but the tribal centers use a similar strategy. The passage moves from tribal centers to a comparison with non-Indigenous institutions. The answer is C.
